title: NASA Satellite Map Shows Stifling Heat Dome Squatting Over the United States
author: Jake Currie
text: Subscribe to skip adsAdvertisementEnvironmentNASA Satellite Map Shows Stifling Heat Dome Squatting Over the United StatesAnd now it’s come for the East CoastJCBy Jake Currie3:00 PM CDT on July 15, 2026Share on FacebookShare on X (formerly Twitter)Share on RedditShare on EmailShare on BlueskyIf you celebrated the Fourth of July outdoors on the East Coast, you’re no stranger to the punishing effects of a heat dome. These weather systems occur when masses of air under high pressure prevent convection and cloud formation, trapping heat in one breeze-less, baking area. Heat domes form slowly and tend to linger, and when coupled with high humidity, they produce a kind of “steam-cooker” effect. For the unfortunate human beings caught inside, they’re sweltering, stifling, and even life-threatening. Subscribe to skip adsFeatured VideoPowered By 10 Scientists Were Able to Revive Pig Brains After an Hour of Death in World FirstShareNextStayRead more: “Extreme Heat Will Change You”While the eastern July 4 heat dome has now passed, there’s another one baking the central United States. NASA recently released an image of temperatures from across the continental U.S. modeled from data recorded by the Goddard Earth Observing System on July 12. It shows the plains states blanketed in oppressive heat, sending temperatures soaring above an estimated 110 degrees Fahrenheit in some regions. Subscribe to skip adsAdvertisement
